extracted_concepts.name: L'Hôpital's Rule
extracted_concepts.definition_snippet: lim f(x)/g(x) = lim f'(x)/g'(x) for 0/0 or ∞/∞ forms

extracted_concepts.name: Taylor's Theorem
extracted_concepts.definition_snippet: f(x) = Σf⁽ᵏ⁾(a)(x-a)ᵏ/k! + R_n with Lagrange remainder

extracted_concepts.name: Maclaurin series
extracted_concepts.definition_snippet: Taylor series centered at 0: e^x = Σxⁿ/n!, sin x = Σ(-1)ⁿx²ⁿ⁺¹/(2n+1)!

extracted_concepts.name: Taylor polynomial
extracted_concepts.definition_snippet: The nth-degree polynomial approximation from Taylor's formula

extracted_concepts.name: convergence of series
extracted_concepts.definition_snippet: Whether the partial sums of an infinite series approach a finite limit

extracted_relations.evidence: L'Hôpital's Rule uses derivatives to compute limits
Taylor's Theorem uses all higher-order derivatives
Maclaurin series is Taylor series at a=0
Taylor polynomial is the polynomial part of Taylor's formula
L'Hôpital's Rule is used to evaluate indeterminate limits
Taylor polynomial generalizes tangent line approximation
